properties.description: Communicate mathematically and approach mathematical situations with precision.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use critical thinking skills to justify mathematical reasoning and critique the reasoning of others.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Identify and utilize structure and patterns.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Reason both contextually and abstractly.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use a variety of mathematical tools effectively and strategically.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Connect mathematical ideas and real-world situations through modeling.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Analyze decisions and strategies using probability concepts.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use probability to evaluate outcomes of decisions. Use probabilities to make fair decisions.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Develop the probability distribution for a random variable defined for a sample space in which a theoretical probability can be calculated and graph the distribution.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Calculate the expected value of a random variable as the mean of its probability distribution. Find expected values by assigning probabilities to payoff values. Use expected values to evaluate and compare strategies in real-world scenarios.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use probability to evaluate outcomes of decisions by finding expected values and determine if decisions are fair.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Construct and compare theoretical and experimental probability distributions and use those distributions to find expected values.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Using technology, create scatterplots and analyze those plots to compare the fit of linear, quadratic, or exponential models to a given data set. Select the appropriate model, fit a function to the data set, and use the function to solve problems in the context of the data.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Select and create an appropriate display, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots, for data that includes only real numbers.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Find linear models using median fit and regression methods to make predictions. Interpret the slope and intercept of a linear model in the context of the data.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Compute using technology and interpret the correlation coefficient of a linear fit.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Summarize and represent data from a single data set. Interpret differences in shape, center, and spread in the context of the data set, accounting for possible effects of extreme data points (outliers).
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use statistics appropriate to the shape of the data distribution to compare center and spread of two or more different data sets that include all real numbers.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Analyze bivariate categorical data using two-way tables and identify possible associations between the two categories using marginal, joint, and conditional frequencies.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use the mean and standard deviation of a data set to fit it to a normal distribution and to estimate population percentages. Recognize that there are data sets for which such a procedure is not appropriate. Use calculators, spreadsheets, and tables to estimate areas under the normal curve.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Create residual plots and analyze those plots to compare the fit of linear, quadratic, and exponential models to a given data set. Select the appropriate model and use it for interpolation.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Differentiate between correlation and causation when describing the relationship between two variables. Identify potential lurking variables which may explain an association between two variables.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use data from a sample survey to estimate a population mean or proportion; develop a margin of error through the use of simulation models for random sampling.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Distinguish between experiments and observational studies. Determine which of two or more possible experimental designs will best answer a given research question and justify the choice based on statistical significance.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand statistics and sampling distributions as a process for making inferences about population parameters based on a random sample from that population.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Distinguish between experimental and theoretical probabilities. Collect data on a chance event and use the relative frequency to estimate the theoretical probability of that event. Determine whether a given probability model is consistent with experimental results.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Plan and conduct a survey to answer a statistical question. Recognize how the plan addresses sampling technique, randomization, measurement of experimental error and methods to reduce bias.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Evaluate claims and conclusions in published reports or articles based on data by analyzing study design and the collection, analysis, and display of the data.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the conditional probability of A given B as P(A and B)/P(B), and interpret independence of A and B as saying that the conditional probability of A given B is the same as the probability of A, and the conditional probability of B given A is the same as the probability of B.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space and use Venn diagrams to represent intersections, unions, and complements.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Recognize and explain the concepts of conditional probability and independence in everyday language and everyday situations.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use the multiplication rule to calculate probabilities for independent and dependent events. Understand that two events A and B are independent if the probability of A and B occurring together is the product of their probabilities, and use this characterization to determine if they are independent.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space and represent sample spaces for compound events using Venn diagrams.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Calculate the conditional probability of an event A given event B as the fraction of B's outcomes that also belong to A, and interpret the answer in terms of the model.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Apply the Addition Rule and the Multiplication Rule to determine probabilities, including conditional probabilities, and interpret the results in terms of the probability model.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Describe events as subsets of a sample space and relate intersections, unions, and complements to the words and, or, and not.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use permutations and combinations to solve mathematical and real-world problems, including determining probabilities of compound events. Justify the results.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Construct and interpret two-way frequency tables of data when two categories are associated with each object being classified. Use the two-way table as a sample space to decide if events are independent and to approximate conditional probabilities.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the concept of the derivative of a function geometrically, numerically, analytically, and verbally.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Apply the rules of differentiation to functions.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Apply theorems and rules of differentiation to solve mathematical and real-world problems.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the concept of a limit graphically, numerically, analytically, and contextually.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the definition and graphical interpretation of continuity of a function.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the concept of the integral of a function geometrically, numerically, analytically, and contextually.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Apply theorems and rules of integration to solve mathematical and real-world problems.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Identify and utilize structure and patterns.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Reason both contextually and abstractly.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use a variety of mathematical tools effectively and strategically.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use critical thinking skills to justify mathematical reasoning and critique the reasoning of others.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Communicate mathematically and approach mathematical situations with precision.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Connect mathematical ideas and real-world situations through modeling.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Convert measurements within a single system of measurement: customary (i.e., in., ft., yd., oz., lb., sec., min., hr.) or metric (i.e., mm, cm, m, km, g, kg, mL, L) from a larger to a smaller unit and a smaller to a larger unit.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the concept of volume measurement.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Create a line plot consisting of unit fractions and use operations on fractions to solve problems related to the line plot.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Differentiate among perimeter, area and volume and identify which application is appropriate for a given situation.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a fraction, improper fraction and a mixed number.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Solve real-world problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions with unlike denominators.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Justify the reasonableness of a product when multiplying with fractions.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand the relationship between fractions and division of whole numbers by interpreting a fraction as the numerator divided by the denominator (i.e., <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi></mfrac></math> = a÷b).
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions and whole numbers, using visual fraction models and equations.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) using a variety of models, including an area model and number line.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Extend the concept of division to divide unit fractions and whole numbers by using visual fraction models and equations.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Extend the concept of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on their attributes.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Plot and interpret points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane to represent real-world and mathematical situations.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Define a coordinate system.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Evaluate numerical expressions involving grouping symbols (i.e., parentheses, brackets, braces).
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Translate verbal phrases into numerical expressions and interpret numerical expressions as verbal phrases.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Investigate the relationship between two numerical patterns.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Connect mathematical ideas and real-world situations through modeling.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Communicate mathematically and approach mathematical situations with precision.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use a variety of mathematical tools effectively and strategically.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use critical thinking skills to justify mathematical reasoning and critique the reasoning of others.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Divide up to a four-digit dividend by a two-digit divisor, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and the relationship between multiplication and division.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use whole number exponents to explain patterns in the number of zeroes of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use whole number exponents to explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimal numbers to hundredths using concrete area models and drawings.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Read and write decimals in standard and expanded form. Compare two decimal numbers to the thousandths using the symbols >, =, or <.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Round decimals to any given place value within thousandths.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using strategies to include a standard algorithm.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand that, in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents 10 times what the same digit represents in the place to its right, and represents <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>10</mi></mfrac></math> times what the same digit represents in the place to its left.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove that all circles are similar.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Derive the formulas for the length of an arc and the area of a sector in a circle and apply these formulas to solve mathematical and real-world problems.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Construct a tangent line to a circle through a point on the circle, and construct a tangent line from a point outside a given circle to the circle; justify the process used for each construction.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Identify and describe relationships among inscribed angles, radii, and chords; among inscribed angles, central angles, and circumscribed angles; and between radii and tangents to circles. Use those relationships to solve mathematical and real-world problems.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Construct the inscribed and circumscribed circles of a triangle using a variety of tools, including a compass, a straightedge, and dynamic geometry software, and prove properties of angles for a quadrilateral inscribed in a circle.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand a dilation takes a line not passing through the center of the dilation to a parallel line, and leaves a line passing through the center unchanged. Verify experimentally the properties of dilations given by a center and a scale factor. Understand the dilation of a line segment is longer or shorter in the ratio given by the scale factor.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove that two triangles are similar using the Angle-Angle criterion and apply the proportionality of corresponding sides to solve problems and justify results.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Understand how the properties of similar right triangles allow the trigonometric ratios to be defined and determine the sine, cosine, and tangent of an acute angle in a right triangle.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems involving similarity about triangles, including a line drawn parallel to one side of a triangle divides the other two sides into parts of equal proportion.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems involving similarity about triangles, including if a line divides two sides of a triangle proportionally, then it is parallel to the third side.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Solve right triangles in applied problems using trigonometric ratios and the Pythagorean Theorem.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Explain and use the relationship between the sine and cosine of complementary angles.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use the definition of similarity to decide if figures are similar and justify decision. Demonstrate that two figures are similar by identifying a combination of translations, rotations, reflections, and dilations in various representations that move one figure onto the other.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use congruence and similarity criteria for triangles to solve problems and to prove relationships in geometric figures.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems involving similarity about triangles, including the square of the hypotenuse of a right triangle is equal to the sum of squares of the other two sides.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use geometric shapes, their measures, and their properties to describe real-world objects.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use geometry concepts and methods to model real-world situations and solve problems using a model.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use statistics appropriate to the shape of the data distribution to compare center and spread of two or more different data sets that include all real numbers.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Summarize and represent data from a single data set. Interpret differences in shape, center, and spread in the context of the data set, accounting for possible effects of extreme data points (outliers).
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Select and create an appropriate display, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots, for data that includes only real numbers.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Explain the derivations of the formulas for the circumference of a circle, area of a circle, and volume of a cylinder, pyramid, and cone. Apply these formulas to solve mathematical and real-world problems.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Explain the derivation of the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures using Cavalieri's principle.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Describe the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects and use those cross-sections to solve mathematical and real-world problems.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Apply surface area and volume formulas for prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres to solve problems and justify results. Include problems that involve algebraic expressions, composite figures, geometric probability, and real-world applications.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use critical thinking skills to justify mathematical reasoning and critique the reasoning of others.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Identify and utilize structure and patterns.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Use a variety of mathematical tools effectively and strategically.

properties.description: Communicate mathematically and approach mathematical situations with precision.

properties.description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Reason both contextually and abstractly.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Connect mathematical ideas and real-world situations through modeling.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about parallelograms, including a parallelograms is a rhombus if and only if the diagonals are perpendicular.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about the relationships within and among triangles, including the segment joining midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side and half the length.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about the relationships within and among triangles, including the medians of a triangle meet at a point.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about parallelograms, including opposite sides of a parallelogram are congruent.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about lines and angles, including any point on a perpendicular bisector of a line segment is equidistant from the endpoints of the segment.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Define angle, perpendicular line, parallel line, line segment, ray, circle, and skew in terms of the undefined notions of point, line, and plane. Use geometric figures to represent and describe real-world objects.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove two triangles are congruent by applying the Side-Angle-Side, Angle-Side-Angle, Angle-Angle-Side, and Hypotenuse-Leg congruence conditions.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Demonstrate that triangles and quadrilaterals are congruent by identifying a combination of translations, rotations, and reflections in various representations that move one figure onto the other.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about the relationships within and among triangles, including base angles of isosceles triangles are congruent.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about lines and angles, including vertical angles are congruent.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Develop definitions of rotations, reflections, and translations in terms of angles, circles, perpendicular lines, parallel lines, and line segments.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about parallelograms, including rectangles are parallelograms with congruent diagonals.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about lines and angles, including perpendicular lines form four right angles.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Construct geometric figures using a variety of tools, including a compass, a straightedge, dynamic geometry software, and paper folding, and use these constructions to make conjectures about geometric relationships.
properties.author: 1EdTech

properties.description: Prove, and apply in mathematical and real-world contexts, theorems about parallelograms, including diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.
